case_id: Regulatory Compliance Matrix for Chlorine, Acetone, Propane, Benzene, Formaldehyde

pi_items.source: EPA List of Regulated Substances under Risk Management Program (40 CFR 68.130); Chlorine is one of 16 chemicals mandatorily included as toxic RMP substance with threshold quantity 2,500 lbs
40 CFR 302.4 Hazardous Substances Table (CAS 7782-50-5); Reportable Quantity 10 lbs
EPA Section 313 Chemical List; Chlorine listed with de minimis limit 1.0%
EPA List of Regulated Substances under Risk Management Program; Acetone is NOT listed on the federal CAA 112r RMP list of 140 regulated substances
40 CFR 302.4 Hazardous Substances Table (CAS 67-64-1); Reportable Quantity 5000 lbs. Acetone is designated as CWA 311 hazardous substance under 40 CFR 116.4
EPA EPCRA Section 313 Chemical List; Acetone is NOT listed as a toxic chemical requiring TRI reporting
EPA List of Regulated Substances under Risk Management Program; Propane is one of 63 flammable RMP substances with threshold quantity 10,000 lbs (CAS 74-98-6)
40 CFR 302.4 Hazardous Substances Table; Propane does NOT appear in the CWA 311 hazardous substances list
EPA EPCRA Section 313 Chemical List; Propane is NOT on the TRI list. Propane is reportable under EPCRA Section 311-312 (Tier II) for inventory purposes only
EPA List of Regulated Substances under Risk Management Program; Benzene is NOT listed on the federal CAA 112r RMP list
40 CFR 302.4 Hazardous Substances Table (CAS 71-43-2); Reportable Quantity 10 lbs. Benzene is a designated CWA 311 hazardous substance
EPA EPCRA Section 313 Chemical List; Benzene is listed on the TRI list (775 chemicals total) and subject to reporting requirements
EPA List of Regulated Substances under Risk Management Program (CAS 50-00-0); Listed as RMP substance with threshold quantity 15,000 lbs
40 CFR 302.4 Hazardous Substances Table (CAS 50-00-0); Reportable Quantity 100 lbs. Formaldehyde is designated as CWA 311 hazardous substance
EPA EPCRA Section 313 Chemical List; Formaldehyde listed with de minimis limit 0.1% and subject to TRI reporting

privileged_info_summary: The regulatory compliance matrix contains one critical error. Four of five chemicals have correct ground truth values: Chlorine (YES, YES, YES), Propane (YES, NO, NO), Benzene (NO, YES, YES), and Formaldehyde (YES, YES, YES) all match EPA regulatory databases. However, Acetone's CWA 311 status is incorrectly listed as 'NO' when the verified value is 'YES' - acetone is designated as a CWA 311 hazardous substance in 40 CFR 302.4 with a reportable quantity of 5000 pounds. Acetone's CAA 112r (NO) and EPCRA 313 (NO) status are correct.
